title: Cryptophoranthus langeanus
taxonomy: Cryptophoranthus langeanus is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Cryptophoranthus and is a type of epiphytic orchid. The family to which it belongs is Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Further classification includes the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Maxillarieae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Cryptophoranthus langeanus, commonly known as the "Lange's Cryptanthus" or "Red Cryptanthus", is a species of plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and compact, rosette-shaped foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Cryptophoranthus langeanus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations.
